title: scaevola calendulacea
intro: Scaevola calendulacea, also known as Yellow Fan Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate, fan-shaped blooms in shades of yellow and white. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including skin irritations and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Scaevola calendulacea include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
